steps.title: Compare old and new evidence
steps.content: Researchers had thought defensive ultrasound was limited to a few moth groups. The new recordings found ultrasonic clicks in 52 genera across many large-bodied moth families.

steps.title: Draw the prevalence claim
steps.content: A discusses other defenses, which the result does not test. B makes an unsupported claim about most moth genera lacking bat defenses. C shifts to nonpredator uses of ultrasound. The new finding shows anti-bat ultrasound may be more widespread than researchers knew. The answer is D.
